title: Coarray Parallelism — The Untapped Multi-Core Frontier
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Fortran coarray parallelism can project 3-4× speedup for batch snap on multi-core hardware
content.proof: Theoretical model based on Amdahl's law. Batch snap parallelizes cleanly across images — each image processes a slice of the input array. Synchronization needed only at final reduction step. Coarray syntax: `real :: data(N)[*]` distributes across images.
content.benchmark: No real benchmark yet. Single-threaded: 2.27B ops/sec. Projected 8-core: ~8-9B ops/sec
content.caveat: UNTESTED. Coarray runtime support varies by compiler. gfortran OpenCoarray runtime has overhead. Intel Fortran ifort/ifx has native support.
